• Bug Catalog

  1. Native graph tools blocked in Plan mode
      - Symptom: GraphSearch shows Denied by permission mode: Plan mode: write operations blocked.
      - Root cause: Plan allowlist includes only GraphQuery, while GraphSearch, GraphContext, GraphNode, etc. are
        native read-only tools.
      - Fix: add all read-only Graph* tool kinds to PermissionMode::Plan.
      - Files: crates/jfc-ui/src/app/permissions.rs:62, crates/jfc-ui/src/tools/defs/graph.rs:113
  2. Tool calls queue until model stream finishes
      - Symptom: Bash/tool calls appear queued and only execute after the assistant finishes emitting its whole
        response.
      - Root cause: handle_stream_tool pushes no-approval tools into app.pending_tool_calls; handle_stream_done is
        the only normal drain point.
      - Fix: dispatch no-approval local tools as soon as their StreamEvent::Tool arrives, or introduce an eager
        batch flush before StreamDone; keep approval/classifier/server-side tools on their existing paths.
      - Files: crates/jfc-ui/src/runtime/event_loop/handlers/stream_tool.rs:167, crates/jfc-ui/src/runtime/
        event_loop/handlers/stream_done.rs:391
  3. Event loop can starve itself while draining queued events
      - Symptom: UI/tool events can look stuck until streaming quiets down.
      - Root cause: the main loop drains rx.try_recv() without a cap; Tokio try_recv frees permits, so producers
        can refill while the loop is still trying to empty the queue.
      - Fix: cap burst draining by count/time, then process/draw; optionally prioritize non-StreamChunk events.
      - File: crates/jfc-ui/src/runtime/event_loop/mod.rs:719
  4. Task auto-completion only works if the model passes parent_task_id
      - Symptom: delegated subagent finishes, but the task list still needs manual TaskUpdate/TaskDone.
      - Root cause: task store updates are linked through TaskInput.parent_task_id; if the model omits it,
        completion only updates the UI agent row.
      - Fix: when a factory/current task is active, inject or infer parent_task_id before dispatching Task; keep
        explicit JSON as an override.
      - Files: crates/jfc-ui/src/runtime/event_loop/handlers/task.rs:260, crates/jfc-ui/src/runtime/factory.rs:122
  5. Task failure cascade uses the agent task id, not the linked todo id
      - Symptom: failed subagent may not cascade/replan against the actual queued task.
      - Root cause: handle_task_failed updates linked_task_id, then calls cascade_failure(task_id.as_str()), where
        task_id is the agent/tool id.
      - Fix: use linked_task_id.unwrap_or(task_id) for task-store cascade/reminder lookup; keep agent id for UI/
        background-agent records.
      - File: crates/jfc-ui/src/runtime/event_loop/handlers/task.rs:347
  6. Task model is partly wired; task effort is not wired
      - Symptom: model override exists, but effort override in Task JSON is unavailable/ignored.
      - Root cause: TaskInput has model, but no effort; AgentDef.effort exists but execute_task_inner never
        applies it to StreamOptions.
      - Fix: add effort to TaskInput, schema, parse/serialize, and session serialization; apply precedence
        Task.effort > AgentDef.effort > global effort via StreamOptions::reasoning_effort.
      - Files: crates/jfc-core/src/task.rs:18, crates/jfc-ui/src/tools/subagent.rs:577, crates/jfc-ui/src/tools/
        defs/agents.rs:76
  7. Plan-mode sed -n ... file false positives
      - Symptom: read-only sed -n '420,470p' path can be denied as sed write/exec.
      - Root cause: sed safety scans every arg, including file paths, as if it were a sed script; paths containing
        s/.../... can trip the naive script parser.
      - Fix: parse sed args enough to distinguish flags/scripts/files; only inspect actual script expressions.
      - File: crates/jfc-ui/src/app/shell_safety.rs:514
  8. Plan-mode find | xargs grep is blocked by blanket xargs denial
      - Symptom: read-only-looking discovery pipelines get denied.
      - Root cause: xargs is classified as a shell-wrapper head and always blocked.
      - Fix option A: keep blocked and steer the model to graph_*, rg, or native tools. Fix option B: implement a
        narrow safe xargs subset for read-only heads only.
      - File: crates/jfc-ui/src/app/shell_safety.rs:404

  Fix Order

  1. Patch Plan-mode allowlist for native graph tools and add tests for every Graph* read-only tool.
  2. Cap event-loop burst draining so queued events are processed predictably.
  3. Change no-approval local tool dispatch from StreamDone-only to eager dispatch.
  4. Make task linkage deterministic by injecting/inferencing parent_task_id.
  5. Fix failure cascade to use the linked task id.
  6. Add Task effort support and apply agent-defined effort.
  7. Tighten sed parsing; decide whether xargs remains blocked policy or gets a safe subset. fix all of the bugs please don't go into plan mode but use the graph tools to idnetify the bugs and fix all of them

The issue is at line 515-519: `sed_script_has_e_modifier` is called on *every* arg, including file paths. A file path containing `s/` (like `/path/to/src/something`) will trip the naive scanner. The fix: identify which args are scripts vs file paths by parsing the sed command line properly.

The sed syntax is: `sed [options] [script] [file...]`
- `-e script` or `--expression=script` explicitly marks a script
- `-f scriptfile` or `--file=scriptfile` marks a script file  
- `-n` is a flag
- The first non-option arg is a script (if no -e was given)
- Subsequent non-option args are file paths
